blurb: Start here before touching memory: the facade, ports/adapters seam, scoping, the self-initializing DB, and the tables.
intent: Orient a future agent in regin's cross-session memory subsystem before touching any of it. `__init__.py` is a thin module-verb facade delegating to a process-wide `SqliteMemoryStore` built with a `SkillRouterEmbedding`. `ports.py` declares the four decoupling interfaces (`EmbeddingProvider`, `LLMProvider`, `MemoryStore`, `MemorySink`), each degrading gracefully; concrete providers live in `adapters.py` at the edge (`SkillRouterEmbedding` with its optional `embed_queries`/`rerank` extensions, `ExternalAgentLLM` over a configured external command, and the `resolve_distiller`/`resolve_topic_classifier`/`resolve_proposal_reviewer` surface binders). `scoping.py` stamps `global`/`repo:<name>`. The single mutable `memories` table carries both tiers via a `tier` column; `models.py` declares its own `memory_metadata` so `regin init`/`rebuild` and Alembic never touch memory tables, and `engine.py` self-initializes a third engine on `db/regin_memory.db`. `mcp_server.py` is the long-lived MCP entrypoint.
label: Agent memory architecture (ports/adapters engine, self-initializing DB, tiers & lifecycle model)
